revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''大西頼武'''（おおにし よりたけ、? - 天正５年（1577年））は、戦国時代の阿波国の[[国人領主]]。阿波[[白地城]]主。出...'
revision.text:
'''大西頼武'''（おおにし よりたけ、? - 天正５年（1577年））は、戦国時代の阿波国の[[国人領主]]。阿波[[白地城]]主。出雲守と称す。弟に元武、子に[[大西覚養|覚養]]、頼晴、[[大西頼包|頼包]]。
大西氏は鎌倉時代に中央より荘官として派遣されてきた[[近藤氏]]が大西郷に依り、改姓したしたものである。 頼武は最初は[[細川氏]]に、後に[[三好長慶]]の妹を娶って阿波西部から讃岐南部、伊予東部、土佐北部で最大勢力を誇った。
やがて[[長宗我部元親]]の四国統一の気運が高まると、四国の中央に位置する白地城はその標的に合うが、そのころ頼武はすでに家督を覚養に譲っていたらしい。
天正５年、元親が阿波侵攻を開始すると、当主の覚養は弟の頼包を人質に出して長宗我部家と和睦を結ぶが、ほどなく三好家の要請に応じて三好側に寝返った。
元親は人質になっていた頼包を案内人として白地城の支城、[[田尾城]]をわずか二日で攻め落とすと、当主の覚養は讃岐へと落ちのびたが、頼武はその過程で自刃したと伝えられる。
== 関連項目 == [[白地城]] [[大西覚養]] [[大西頼包]] [[三好長慶]] [[長宗我部元親]]

天正５年、元親が阿波侵攻を開始すると、当主の覚養は弟の頼包を人質に出して長宗我部家と和睦を結ぶが、ほどなく三好家の要請に応じて三好側に寝返った。
元親は人質になっていた頼包を案内人として白地城の支城、[[田尾城]]をわずか二日で攻め落とすと、当主の覚養は讃岐へと落ちのびたが、頼武はその過程で自刃したと伝えられる。

{{出典の明記|date=2009年12月}} '''大西頼武'''（おおにし よりたけ、? - 天正５年（1577年））は、戦国時代の阿波国の[[国人領主]]。阿波[[白地城]]主。出雲守と称す。弟に元武、子に[[大西覚養|覚養]]、頼晴、[[大西頼包|頼包]]。
== 生涯 ==
大西氏は鎌倉時代に中央より荘官として派遣されてきた[[近藤氏]]が大西郷に依り、改姓したものである。 頼武は最初は[[細川氏]]に属し、後に[[三好長慶]]の妹を娶って阿波西部から讃岐南部、伊予東部、土佐北部で最大勢力を誇った。
やがて[[長宗我部元親]]の四国統一の気運が高まると、四国の中央に位置する白地城はその標的にあうが、そのころ頼武はすでに家督を覚養に譲っていたらしい。
天正５年、元親が阿波侵攻を開始すると、当主の覚養は弟の頼包を人質に出して長宗我部家と和睦を結ぶが、ほどなく三好家の要請に応じて三好側に寝返った。
元親は人質になっていた頼包を案内人として白地城の支城、[[田尾城]]をわずか二日で攻め落とすと、当主の覚養は讃岐へと落ちのびたが、頼武はその過程で自刃したと伝えられる。

{{出典の明記|date=2009年12月}} '''大西 頼武'''（おおにし よりたけ、? - [[天正]]5年（[[1577年]]））は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[阿波国]]の[[国人領主]]。阿波[[白地城]]主。出雲守と称す。弟に元武、子に[[大西覚養|覚養]]、頼晴、[[大西頼包|頼包]]。
== 生涯 == [[大西氏]]は[[鎌倉時代]]に中央より荘官として派遣されてきた[[近藤氏]]が大西郷に依り、改姓したものである。 頼武は最初は[[細川氏]]に属し、後に[[三好長慶]]の妹を娶って阿波西部から[[讃岐国|讃岐]]南部、[[伊予国|伊予]]東部、[[土佐国|土佐]]北部で最大勢力を誇った。
やがて[[長宗我部元親]]の[[四国]]統一の気運が高まると、四国の中央に位置する白地城はその標的にあうが、そのころ頼武はすでに家督を覚養に譲っていたらしい。
天正5年、元親が阿波侵攻を開始すると、当主の覚養は弟の頼包を人質に出して長宗我部家と和睦を結ぶが、ほどなく[[三好氏]]の要請に応じて三好側に寝返った。
元親は人質になっていた頼包を案内人として白地城の支城、[[田尾城]]をわずか二日で攻め落とすと、当主の覚養は讃岐へと落ちのびたが、頼武はその過程で自刃したと伝えられる。
== 関連項目 == *[[白地城]] *[[大西覚養]] *[[大西頼包]] *[[三好長慶]] *[[長宗我部元親]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōnishi Yoritake}} [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Sengoku samurai]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:People of Awa Province]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:1577 deaths]]

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Sengoku samurai]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:People of Awa Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Onishi Yoritake}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:1577 deaths]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]]

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.

revision.comment: adding defaultsort to all of them
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also! == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|12=draft}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国|12=draft}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=draft}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=draft}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=draft}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=draft}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=draft}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏|12=draft}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|12=draft}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|12=draft}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国|12=draft}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国|12=draft}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏|12=draft}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=draft}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft}}

The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏|12=draft|12=draft}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=draft|12=draft}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft|12=draft}}

{{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 ({{ill|1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|ja|細川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|ja|伊予国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|ja|土佐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|ja|四国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|ja|三好氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大西頼武 up to rev 45510548
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2009年12月}} '''大西 頼武'''（おおにし よりたけ、? - [[天正]]5年（[[1577年]]））は、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の[[阿波国]]の[[国人領主]]。阿波[[白地城]]主。出雲守と称す。弟に元武、子に[[大西覚養|覚養]]、頼晴、[[大西頼包|頼包]]。
== 生涯 == [[大西氏]]は[[鎌倉時代]]に中央より荘官として派遣されてきた[[近藤氏]]が大西郷に依り、改姓したものである。 頼武は最初は[[細川氏]]に属し、後に[[三好長慶]]の妹を娶って阿波西部から[[讃岐国|讃岐]]南部、[[伊予国|伊予]]東部、[[土佐国|土佐]]北部で最大勢力を誇った。
やがて[[長宗我部元親]]の[[四国]]統一の気運が高まると、四国の中央に位置する白地城はその標的にあうが、そのころ頼武はすでに家督を覚養に譲っていたらしい。
天正5年、元親が阿波侵攻を開始すると、当主の覚養は弟の頼包を人質に出して長宗我部家と和睦を結ぶが、ほどなく[[三好氏]]の要請に応じて三好側に寝返った。
元親は人質になっていた頼包を案内人として白地城の支城、[[田尾城]]をわずか2日で攻め落とすと、当主の覚養は讃岐へと落ちのびたが、頼武はその過程で自刃したと伝えられる。
== 関連項目 == * [[白地城]] * [[大西覚養]] * [[大西頼包]] * [[三好長慶]] * [[長宗我部元親]]
{{デフォルトソート:おおにし よりたけ}} [[Category:戦国武将]] [[Category:阿波国の人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:1577年没]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|death_date=1577|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|qid=Q11545923}}|spouse={{ill|Q108771865|qid=Q108771865}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438939]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Yoritake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 ([[:en:1577|1577]])) was a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|qq=|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|qq=|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]]. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]] at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|qq=|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|qq=|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|qq=|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1577|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}|spouse={{ill|Q108771865|WD=Q108771865}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438939]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Yoritake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 ([[:en:1577|1577]])) was a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]]. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]] at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.

Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]

His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]]. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]] at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1577|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}|spouse={{ill|Q108771865|WD=Q108771865}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438939]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Yoritake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 ([[:en:1577|1577]])) was a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]]. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]] at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.

This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.

Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1577|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}|spouse={{ill|Q108771865|WD=Q108771865}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438939]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Yoritake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 ([[:en:1577|1577]])) was a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of [[:en:Awa Province (Tokushima)|Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the [[:en:Hosokawa clan|Hosokawa clan]] but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]]. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], eastern [[:en:Iyo Province|Iyo Province]], and northern [[:en:Tosa Province|Tosa Province]].
As [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]] began his campaign to unify [[:en:Shikoku|Shikoku]], the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the [[:en:Miyoshi clan|Miyoshi clan]] at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]] * [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chōsokabe Motochika]]
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大西頼武]] {{Infobox person|death_date=1577|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|occupation={{ill|military commander|WD=Q11545923}}|spouse={{ill|Q108771865|WD=Q108771865}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438939]] Wikidata name: Ōnishi Yoritake [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=December 2009}} '''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 ({{ill|1577|en|1577|WD=Q6704|lt=1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Kondō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|en|Shikoku|WD=Q13991|lt=Shikoku}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Tao Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Ōnishi Yoritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika}}
{{translated page|ja|大西頼武|version=45510548|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Life == The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Q11638843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.
Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan|1=Hosokawa clan}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}.
As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|en|Shikoku|WD=Q13991|lt=Shikoku|1=Shikoku}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.
In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}} at their request.
Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Q11576557|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict. == See Also == * {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Q11438937|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}} * {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}} * {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}

== Life ==
The {{ill|Ōnishi clan|ja|大西氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} originated during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, when the {{ill|Kondō clan|ja|近藤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō clan|1=Q11638843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638843}}, sent as estate stewards from the central government, settled in the Ōnishi region and adopted the Ōnishi name.

Yoritake initially served the {{ill|Hosokawa clan|en|Hosokawa clan|WD=Q2736120|lt=Hosokawa clan|1=Hosokawa clan}} but later strengthened his influence by marrying the sister of {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi|1=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}. This alliance enabled the Ōnishi clan to dominate western Awa, southern {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, eastern {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|WD=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province|1=Iyo Province}}, and northern {{ill|Tosa Province|en|Tosa Province|WD=Q857330|lt=Tosa Province|1=Tosa Province}}.

As {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}} began his campaign to unify {{ill|Shikoku|en|Shikoku|WD=Q13991|lt=Shikoku|1=Shikoku}}, the strategically located Hakuchi Castle became a primary target. By this time, Yoritake had reportedly passed leadership of the clan to his son, Kakuyō.

In {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 (1577), Motochika launched his invasion of Awa. The current head, Kakuyō, initially sought peace by offering his brother, Yoritomo, as a hostage. However, he soon defected to the {{ill|Miyoshi clan|en|Miyoshi clan|WD=Q2975329|lt=Miyoshi clan|1=Miyoshi clan}} at their request.

Motochika responded by capturing {{ill|Tao Castle|ja|田尾城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tao Castle|1=Q11576557|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11576557}}, a key fortification of Hakuchi Castle, within just two days, guided by the hostage Yoritomo. While Kakuyō fled to Sanuki, Yoritake is said to have committed seppuku during the conflict.

'''Ōnishi Yoritake''' (大西 頼武, ? - {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 5 ({{ill|1577|en|1577|WD=Q6704|lt=1577|1=1577}})) was a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} {{ill|local lord|ja|国人領主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local lord|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国人|1=kokujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q859955}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province (Tokushima)|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}. He was the lord of {{ill|Hakuchi Castle|ja|白地城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi Castle|qq=}} and held the title Izumo-no-kami. His brothers included Mototake, and his children were {{ill|Ōnishi Kakuyō|ja|大西覚養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Kakuyō|qq=}}, Yoriharu, and {{ill|Ōnishi Yoritomo|ja|大西頼包|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi Yoritomo|1=Q11438937|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438937}}.
